prompt: Create a highly realistic studio creature concept image of a hybrid quadruped resembling a powerful black bull or bison with a massive muscular body, short dark fur, heavy folded skin around the neck, sturdy legs, split hooves, a thin tail with a tuft, and a raised shoulder hump. The creature’s head is transformed into a delicate white deer-like face enclosed inside a large perfectly transparent glass sphere, like a biological helmet or orb. Inside the sphere, show an intricate luminous branching antler system: countless ivory-white coral-like antler branches radiate upward and outward, with translucent wet organic textures, tiny pearl-like nodes, and fine vein-like tendrils continuing back into the black shoulder area. The visible head should have a calm pale face, small ears, dark eyes, and a narrow muzzle, contrasting sharply with the dark body. Add exactly one main creature, one transparent spherical helmet, one pale deer-like head, one large branching antler crown, four visible legs, and one tail. Use deep charcoal black, porcelain white, and neutral light gray. Compose the creature in a full-body three-quarter side view facing right, centered on a seamless gray studio backdrop with soft floor contact shadow. Lighting should be soft, diffused, and high-end photographic, emphasizing glossy glass reflections, velvety fur, sculptural anatomy, and subtle biomechanical organic details. Style: hyperrealistic cinematic creature design, museum specimen photography, elegant surrealism, no text, no humans, no extra animals, no environment props.
